加拿大首个主权财富基金

Canada's first sovereign wealth fund

www.cbc.ca 作者 geox 分数 140 评论 99 2026/04/28 00:58
打开 HN 原帖 打开原始文章

中文摘要

  1. 政治 总理马克·卡尼 (Mark Carney) 公布了创建加拿大首个主权财富基金的计划。该基金将用于资助国家利益重大项目的建设彼得·泽蒙季奇 (Peter Zimonjic) · CBC 新闻 · 发布时间： 2026 年 4 月 27 日上午 7:39 EDT |最后更新：6小时前收听这篇文章预计6分钟本文的音频版本是基于AI技术生成的。可能会出现发音错误。我们正在与我们的合作伙伴合作，不断审查和改进结果。马克·卡尼总理宣布设立一个主权财富基金，初始捐赠额为 250 亿美元，他表示这将为子孙后代创造财富。 （加通社/阿德里安·维尔德）加拿大总理马克·卡尼宣布计划创建加拿大首个主权财富基金。卡尼在网上发布的视频中表示，“加拿大强大基金”将作为投资工具，为涉及国家利​​益的重大项目提供资金，并将与私营部门合作。卡尼在视频中表示，加拿大人将能够向该基金捐款并从中受益，与私营部门和国际合作伙伴一起投资。他说：“如果您有一点额外的钱，我们将让您轻松投资该基金，以帮助加拿大为所有人建设强大的国家。”联邦政府在一份声明中表示，该基金将包括“清洁和常规能源、关键矿产、农业和基础设施”领域的项目。卡尼公布了他希望快速推进的重大项目，包括新矿山、液化天然气和水力开发。联邦政府的重大项目办公室将做什么——它会成功吗？跨山董事会主席将领导设在卡尔加里的新联邦重大项目办公室。卡尼周一在渥太华加拿大科学技术博物馆举行的新闻发布会上表示，该基金将以初始项目开始捐赠250亿美元。卡尼表示：“随着时间的推移，该基金将通过资产回收和再投资实现增长，为子孙后代创造更大的机会。”当被问及考虑到加拿大的财政状况，这250亿加元将来自哪里时，卡尼表示，周二的春季经济更新将带来这样的消息：加拿大的财政状况比卡尼政府预计刚刚结束的财政年度赤字783亿加元时更加强劲。“为了让数字更好，你必须超越它们，而我们也做到了这一点，”卡尼说。由于伊朗、美国和以色列之间的战争，最近石油价格飙升，增加了加拿大产油省份的收入，进而增加了联邦政府的收入。卡尼表示，这次会有所不同。卡尼表示，加拿大强大基金将由一家独立的皇家公司管理，该公司将向议会报告，他的政府将在接下来的几个月里就“该基金的具体方面”进行咨询。卡尼将该基金描述为“本质上是一个国民储蓄和投资账户”，该基金的设计目的是“为子孙后代增加财富”。“这将是加拿大政府的基金，但更重要的是，这将是一个人民的基金。它将是你的基金，”卡尼说。为了让人们向该基金捐款，联邦政府将推出一种“零售投资产品”，例如共同基金或养老金计划，加拿大人可以购买该基金并赚取股息。政府在一份声明中表示：“这将使加拿大人直接参与我们国家的长期繁荣，并有助于建立长期的国家财富。”观看 |总理宣布创建国家主权财富基金的计划：卡尼宣布加拿大首个主权财富基金7小时前|持续时间2:07总理马克·卡尼宣布创建加拿大首个主权财富基金。卡尼表示，加拿大强大基金将与私营部门合作，为重大国家建设项目提供资金。卡尼还表示，与加拿大太平洋铁路一样，他的政府试图建设的重大项目将主要由私营公司建造。就像 1870 年代一样，卡尼表示“联邦政府将通过贷款、赠款和其他激励措施支持这些项目。”卡尼唤起了定义加拿大历史的重大基础设施项目的精神，但他表示，拟议的计划将不同于加拿大太平洋铁路等项目，加拿大太平洋铁路是通过将原住民从他们的土地上赶走而建造的，那里的工人在“恶劣的条件”下劳动，唯一从这些项目中受益的人是建造这些项目的公司。卡尼表示，原住民将通过股权成为这些项目的全面合作伙伴；资助的项目将由从事“高薪工会工作”的加拿大人建造；由于政府投资该基金，所有加拿大人，无论是直接投资还是作为纳税人间接投资，都将受益。国家级项目去年 6 月，议会通过了卡尼 C-5 法案，旨在加快对被认定为“国家建设”的重大基础设施项目的审批。该法案的后半部分，即《加拿大建筑法》，使联邦内阁能够挑选项目，预先批准这些项目，并凌驾于联邦法律、环境审查和许可程序之上。该法案通过引入“一个项目、一个审查”的方法，而不是依次进行联邦和省级审批程序，将审批时间从五年缩短到两年。Poilievre 要求卡尼将联邦赤字上限限制在春季经济更新前$31B赤字低于预期，春季财政更新中预计将有新的负担能力措施 自由党将在全球不稳定的情况下于4月28日提交春季经济更新卡尼去年8月创建的重大项目办公室（MPO）是对重大项目进行宣传、投诉或表达担忧的中心场所。卡尼在宣布MPO时表示，它将“帮助构建和协调私营部门、省和地区合作伙伴的融资”以及联邦政府，以确保纳税人得到的是物有所值的。卡尼表示，通过该基金资助的项目将不仅限于涉及国家利​​益的项目，这些项目必须满足某些基准才能获得该分类。“根据我的判断，不会仅限于此。我们将就具体细节进行磋商，因此它的范围比专门的 C-5 更广泛，”他周一表示。普利耶夫批评卡尼设立债务融资基金 保守党领袖皮埃尔·普利耶夫批评卡尼用借来的钱资助加拿大强大基金，称各国需要有财富来建立财富基金，但加拿大只有债务。 “投资是存在的，它来自我们国家，”波利耶夫说。 “这在我们国家根本无法获得回报。在国家信用卡上再投入250​​亿美元来填补自由党的行贿基金并不会改变这一点。” Poilievre表示，加拿大的项目很难从私营部门获得资金和投资，因为繁琐的法规和法律阻碍了发展，而不是因为现金短缺。 “如果一个项目有商业案例，为什么政府需要资助它？如果没有商业案例，政府为什么要资助它？”普利耶夫周一问道。作者简介 Peter Zimonjic 是 CBC 新闻的资深撰稿人，负责数字、广播和电视报道。他曾在英国伦敦为《电讯报》、《泰晤士报》和《每日邮报》担任记者和专栏作家，在加拿大为《渥太华公民》、《Torstar》和《太阳媒体》担任记者和专栏作家。他是《Into The Darkness: An Account of 7/7》一书的作者，该书由 Vintage 出版。

Original Summary

  1. PoliticsPrime Minister Mark Carney has unveiled his plan to create Canada's first sovereign wealth fund.Fund will be used to finance construction of major projects of national interestPeter Zimonjic · CBC News · Posted: Apr 27, 2026 7:39 AM EDT | Last Updated: 6 hours agoListen to this articleEstimated 6 minutesThe audio version of this article is generated by AI-based technology. Mispronunciations can occur. We are working with our partners to continually review and improve the results.Prime Minister Mark Carney announced the creation of a sovereign wealth fund with an initial endowment of $25 billion that he says will create wealth for future generations. (The Canadian Press/Adrian Wyld)Prime Minister Mark Carney has announced his plan to create Canada's first sovereign wealth fund.The "Canada Strong Fund" will serve as an investment vehicle to finance major projects of national interest and will work in partnership with the private sector, Carney said in a video posted online. Carney said in the video that Canadians will be able to contribute to and benefit from the fund, investing alongside the private sector and international partners. "If you have a bit of extra money, we'll make it easy for you to invest in the fund to help build Canada strong for all," he said. In a statement, the federal government said the fund will include projects in "clean and conventional energy, critical minerals, agriculture, and infrastructure."Carney unveils major projects he wants fast-tracked, including new mines, LNG and hydro developmentWhat will the federal government's Major Projects Office do — and will it succeed?Trans Mountain board chair to lead new federal Major Projects Office based in CalgaryAt Monday's press conference at the Canadian Science and Technology Museum in Ottawa, Carney said the fund will begin with an initial endowment of $25 billion. "Over time, the fund will grow through asset recycling and reinvestment, creating even greater opportunities for future generations," Carney said. When asked where that $25 billion will come from considering Canada's fiscal situation, Carney said the Spring Economic Update on Tuesday will deliver news that Canada's finances are on a stronger footing than they were when Carney's government projected a $78.3-billion deficit for the fiscal year just ended."In order for the numbers to be better, you have to be on top of them, and we are on top of them," Carney said. The recent spike in the price of oil, due to the war between Iran and the United States and Israel, has boosted revenues in Canada's oil-producing provinces and in turn boosted how much revenue the federal government has collected. This time will be different, Carney saysCarney said the Canada Strong Fund will be managed by an arm's-length independent Crown corporation that will report to Parliament, and his government will spend the next few months consulting on "specific aspects of the fund."Describing the fund as "essentially a national savings and investment account," Carney said the fund is being designed to "grow wealth for future generations.""This will be a Government of Canada fund, but more importantly, this will be a people’s fund. It will be your fund," Carney said. In order to allow people to contribute to the fund, the federal government will launch a "retail investment product" like a mutual fund or pension scheme where Canadians can buy into the fund and earn a dividend. "This will give Canadians a direct stake in our nation’s long-term prosperity and help build long-term national wealth," the government said in a statement. WATCH | PM announces plan to create national sovereign wealth fund:Carney announces Canada's first sovereign wealth fund7 hours ago|Duration 2:07Prime Minister Mark Carney has announced the creation of Canada's first sovereign wealth fund. Carney says the Canada Strong Fund will finance major nation-building projects in partnership with the private sector.Carney also said that like the Canadian Pacific Railway, the major projects his government is trying to get built will mostly be constructed by private companies. Just like in the 1870s, Carney said "the federal government will support these projects through loans, grants and other incentives."Carney evoked the spirit of major infrastructure projects that defined Canada's history, but said the proposed plans would be different from things like the Canadian Pacific Railway, which was built by displacing Indigenous peoples from their land, where workers laboured under "appalling conditions" and the only people who benefitted from the projects were the companies that built them. Carney said Indigenous peoples will be full partners in the projects through equity stakes; that the projects being financed will be built by Canadians in "high-paying union jobs"; and because the government is investing in the fund, all Canadians, whether they invest directly or indirectly as taxpayers, will benefit. Projects of national interestBill C-5, Carney's legislation to speed up approvals for major infrastructure projects identified as being "nation-building," passed through Parliament last June.The second half of the bill, the Building Canada Act, enables the federal cabinet to pick projects, approve them upfront and override federal laws, environmental reviews and the permitting process.The legislation speeds approval times from five years to two by introducing a "one project, one review" approach instead of having federal and provincial approval processes happen sequentially.Poilievre demands Carney cap federal deficit at $31B ahead of spring economic updateLower-than-forecasted deficit, new affordability measures expected in spring fiscal updateLiberals to table spring economic update April 28 amid global instabilityThe Major Projects Office (MPO), which Carney created last August, is the central place to make pitches, deliver complaints or express concerns about major projects.When he announced the MPO, Carney said it would "help structure and co-ordinate financing from the private sector, provincial and territorial partners" and the federal government to ensure taxpayers get value for money. Carney said the projects being financed through the fund will not be limited to ones of national interest, which have to meet certain benchmarks to get that classification."It wouldn't be restricted to that, in my judgement. We'll consult on the specifics of that, so it's a broader range than would be just specifically C-5," he said on Monday. Poilievre criticizes Carney for debt-financing fundConservative Leader Pierre Poilievre criticized Carney for using borrowed money to endow the Canada Strong Fund, saying countries need to have wealth for a wealth fund, but all Canada has is debt. "The investment exists, it comes from our country," Poilievre said. "It just can't get a return in our country. Putting another $25 billion on the national credit card to pad a Liberal slush fund will not change that."Poilievre said projects in Canada are struggling to get funding and investment from the private sector because of onerous regulations and laws that frustrate development, not because of a shortage of cash. "If a project has a business case, why would the government need to fund it? If it doesn't have a business case, why would the government want to fund it?" Poilievre asked Monday. ABOUT THE AUTHORPeter Zimonjic is a senior writer for CBC News who reports for digital, radio and television. He has worked as a reporter and columnist in London, England, for the Telegraph, Times and Daily Mail, and in Canada for the Ottawa Citizen, Torstar and Sun Media. He is the author of Into The Darkness: An Account of 7/7, published by Vintage.

这是一个主权财富基金，你抱怨其中一家较大的加拿大公司被纳入其中，因为卡尼在那里工作。然后，当被追问时，你说你希望其他人代表你的人民（假设你是加拿大人）。

petermcneeley 2026/04/28 12:08
> 也许我会简单地让不同的人代表加拿大人。这个要求是否太过分了？

这是指卡尼。我认为卡尼不能很好地代表人民。

它不是主权财富基金。这个名字的目的是与像挪威这样的真正的主权财富基金发生冲突。基本上你需要财富，而加拿大有财富的反面，它有赤字。

该基金（当然）是通过债务创建的。这有点像借钱投资股市，而不是尝试投资你的储蓄/收入。由于管理不善，加拿大已经背负了巨额联邦债务。

那么加拿大为什么要设立这样一个奇怪的“基金”呢？当你让一位银行家掌管一个国家时，就会发生这样的情况。您可以获得解决国家问题的银行解决方案。

现在你明白为什么卡尼的职业道路很重要了。 这只是冰山一角。如果您想了解更多信息，我可以将您链接到您可能会感兴趣的许多方面。

> Perhaps I would have simply had someone different represent Canadians. Is this too much to ask?

This was in reference to Carney. I dont think Carney represents the people very well.

It is not a sovereign wealth fund. The name is intended to clash with a real sovereign wealth fund like say Norway has. Basically you need wealth and Canada has the inverse of wealth it has deficits.

The fund was created (of course) by debt. It is a bit like borrowing to invest in the stock market rather than trying to invest your savings/income. Canada already has massive federal debt due to insane levels of mismanagment.

So why would Canada create such a bizarre 'fund'. Well it is what happens when you put a banker in charge of a country. You get banking solutions to country problems.

So now you see why Carney's career path matters. This is just the tip of the iceberg. If you want to know more I can link you to many facets that you might find interesting.

在 HN 中查看
adjejmxbdjdn 2026/04/28 04:48
卡尼在金融危机期间担任加拿大央行行长，加拿大是受影响最小的发达国家之一。公平地说，加拿大孤立的银行体系也有助于限制风险，但卡尼对局势的处理足够好，英国任命他为英格兰银行第一位非英国行长，在此期间他监督了整个英国脱欧传奇，他对英国经济的处理再次受到高度评价。

关于他是否会成为一名优秀的总理存在合理的疑问，但他可能是有史以来最成功的央行行长之一。

Carney was the governor of the Bank of Canada through the financial crisis and Canada was one of the least affected developed countries. To be fair, Canada’s insular banking system also helped limit exposure, but Carney’s handling of the situation was good enough that the UK made him the first non British governor of the Bank of England, during which period he oversaw the whole Brexit saga, where again his handling of the UK economy was extremely highly regarded.

There are justifiable question about whether he will be a good PM but he is probably one of the most successful central bankers of all time.

boringg 2026/04/28 01:33
如今最大的挑战之一是寻找可投资的项目。 如果他们把这笔钱作为对项目时间的保留，那么对加拿大来说可能是一笔很好的未来资产。 如果它最终实际上更多地成为政治利益的就业创造工具，那么可能就不那么成功了。
One of the biggest challenges is finding investable projects these days. If they put this money in as a hold to time projects well than could be a good future asset for Canada. If it ends up actually being more of a jobs production vehicle for political gain then probably less successful.

他们会用债务来资助他们的“财富”吗？

这是一个矛盾的说法。

如果你拥有 100 万美元但欠 400 万美元，那么你就不算“富有”。 你是一个生活在谎言中的骗子，最终会把你压垮。

顺便说一句，如果你有 - 300 万美元，抱歉，但你是我最不想投资的人......

挪威拥有财富基金，因为他们人口少，石油收入却很高，而且他们不是由白痴经营的。

加拿大的石油产量大约是挪威的两倍，但人口却是挪威的十倍。 抱歉，除非您开始多开采 5 倍的石油，否则你们不可能都像挪威人一样富有。

像这样的事情应该在世界舞台上被嘲笑。

我们生活在一个颠倒的世界。

How are you going to have a Sovereign Wealth Fund when you're in debt ~300% to GDP?

Are they going to fund their "wealth" with debt?

This is an oxymoron.

You aren't "rich" if you have $1M and you owe $4M. You're a con-man living a lie that will crush you eventually.

And by the way, if you have -$3M, sorry, but you're the last person I want to invest money with...

Norway gets to have a wealth fund because they have a small population with a massive amount of oil revenue, and they aren't run by morons.

Canada only produces about 2x as much oil as Norway, but it's got 10x the population. Sorry, you can't all be rich like Norwegians unless you start pumping 5x more oil.

Things like this should be laughed off the world stage.

We live in an upside down world.

lesuorac 2026/04/28 01:42
如果波动性低于市场，为什么很重要？

短期内的未来支付由资金流入来支付。

你不妨现在就最大化回报，这样在未来当它没有被资金流入覆盖时，你就能获得更大的回报。

Why does it matter if volatility is lower than the market?

Future payments in the short term are covered by inflows.

You might as well maximize the returns now so that in the future when it's not covered by inflows you've acrewed a larger return.

在 HN 中查看
jjtheblunt 2026/04/28 01:57
> 短期内的未来支付由资金流入来支付。

但这与庞氏骗局模式相似吗？

> Future payments in the short term are covered by inflows.

is that similar to the Ponzi scheme pattern, though?

在 HN 中查看
AnthonyMouse 2026/04/28 03:08
庞氏骗局 总是 从当前流入中支付当前付款。前 10 个人从接下来的 100 个人的流入中获得报酬，然后从接下来的 1000 人中获得报酬，依此类推，直到没有人可以报名，最后一组就只剩下包袱了。这就是美国社会保障的运作方式，因为它一开始是向从未缴款的人付款，并以 20 世纪早期的生育率 >3.5 而不是目前的 1.6 为前提，以防止系统崩溃，这就是“信托基金”资金耗尽的原因——它一开始就没有足够的资金来支付未来的付款。

然而，在个别年份，基金支付的利息超过其收取的利息，只要这不是平均发生的情况，就不是问题。

Ponzi schemes always make current payments out of current inflows. The first 10 people get paid from the inflows from the next 100 people who get paid from the next 1000 people and so on, until you run out of people to sign up and the last group is left holding the bag. This is how Social Security works in the US because it started out by making payments to people who never paid in and was premised on the early 20th century fertility rate of >3.5 instead of the current ~1.6 to keep the system from collapsing, which is why the "trust fund" is running out of money -- it never had enough to cover future payments to begin with.

Whereas having individual years when the fund pays out more than it collected in interest is not a problem as long as that's not what happens on average.

vkou 2026/04/28 03:04
> 为什么波动性低于市场水平很重要？

因为我可以 琐碎地 以 3:1 的保证金做多，跑赢市场约 100%。

波动性就是为什么这是一个坏主意。五分之一的投资策略的结果是“市场崩溃了，我输了” 一切'。

对于一个管理良好的国家来说，“哈哈，YOLO”并不是一个很好的投资策略。

> Why does it matter if volatility is lower than the market?

Because I can trivially beat the market by ~100% by going long on 3:1 margin.

The volatility is why that's a bad idea. One time out of five, the consequence of that investment strategy is 'The market had a crash and I lose everything'.

'Lol, YOLO' is not a great investment strategy for a well-ran country.

在 HN 中查看
AnthonyMouse 2026/04/28 03:21
> 五分之一的投资策略的结果是“市场崩溃了，我输了” 一切'。

这就是为什么该策略实际上并没有击败市场。如果继续使用它30年，你就会破产。

然而，如果你在30年前，甚至50年前，甚至50年前，把钱投入主要指数，然后放在那里，结果会怎样呢？如果你在 1926 年把所有的钱投入市场并在那里待了 100 年，你的处境还会很糟糕吗？

> One time out of five, the consequence of that investment strategy is 'The market had a crash and I lose everything'.

Which is why that strategy doesn't actually beat the market. Keep using it for 30 years and you're bankrupt.

Whereas if you put your money in a major index 30 years ago and left it there, or even 50 or more years ago, what result? Are you even in a bad place if you put all your money into the market in 1926 and left it there for 100 years?

在 HN 中查看
akamaka 2026/04/28 06:00
是的，如果退休基金在 1926 年将所有资金投入股票指数，那么它就无法在整个 30 年代和 1940 年代支付养老金，并且在市场最终复苏之前就已经破产。

对于20-50岁的个人来说，全指数化是一个很好的策略，但对于需要持续支付的养老基金来说却不是一个策略。

Yes, if a retirement fund had put all their money into a stock index in 1926, it wouldn’t have been able to pay out pensions throughout the 1930s and 1940s and would have been bankrupt before the market eventually recovered.

Going full index is a great strategy for an individual person aged 20-50, but not a strategy for a pension fund which needs to continuously pay out.

在 HN 中查看
roncesvalles 2026/04/28 02:38
“我们取得了优异的风险调整回报”作为主权基金表现不佳的借口纯属无稽之谈。私募股权（取决于其杠杆程度）本质上比购买公开股票的波动性更低。

如果你的基金获得的回报持续低于你将所有资金投入 60/40 投资组合的回报，那么整个努力就失败了。

"We achieved superior risk-adjusted returns" as an excuse for sovereign fund underperformance is nonsense. PE (depending on how levered it is) inherently has lower volatility than buying public stocks.

If your fund gets consistently lower returns than if you had just stuck everything in a 60/40 portfolio, the whole endeavor has failed.

在 HN 中查看
triceratops 2026/04/28 02:54
我真的很喜欢把一切都扔到 VTI 的理想（或者，因为它是加拿大，所以是其他类似的东西）。但在这种规模下它仍然有效吗？或者基金是否对相关指数施加了自己的引力场？
I really like the ideal of just chucking it all in VTI (or, since it's Canada, some other equivalent). But does it still work at that scale? Or does the fund exert its own gravitational field on the index in question?
在 HN 中查看
AnthonyMouse 2026/04/28 03:16
指标引力场这么大是原因​​之一 为什么 有用。当一家公司被纳入主要指数时，其股价通常会上涨，因为现在有更多的人试图将其作为指数的一部分购买。

名义上的风险是，如果您想将如此大的基金转移到其他投资中，出售行为会降低基金中资产的价格。但无论你将这笔钱投资于什么，都会发生这种情况。但是，分布广泛的整个市场指数往往会 缓解 那。

真正的问题在于，它将人们的投资与公司的基本面脱节。有前途的公司如果不在指数中就不会获得那么多投资，而管理不善的公司如果在指数中就会获得太多投资。

The gravitational field of indexes that large is one of the reasons why it works. The stock price of a company will generally increase when it's added to a major index because there are now so many more people trying to buy it as part of the index.

The risk is nominally that if you ever wanted to move a fund that large into some other investments, the act of selling would lower the price of the assets in the fund. But that's what happens no matter what you invest that amount of money in. But then widely distributed whole-market indexes would tend to mitigate that.

The real problem with this is that it disconnects what people invest in from the fundamentals of the companies. Promising companies don't get as much investment if they're not in an index, and mismanaged companies get too much if they are.

在 HN 中查看
triceratops 2026/04/28 07:21
> 索引引力场这么大是它起作用的原因之一

> The gravitational field of indexes that large is one of the reasons why it works

I'm confused because my question was whether a sovereign wealth fund could move an index by too much. Not about the issues with index investing (which IMO are mostly overblown).

在 HN 中查看
bparsons 2026/04/28 01:51
在过去十年（甚至更早）中，CPPIB 一直是同类基金中表现最好的。国家养老基金与个人投资组合或家族办公室具有不同的风险承受能力和投资准则。

感谢 CPPIB，加拿大没有巨额无资金准备的养老金负债（与我们南方的邻国不同）。这是一个巨大的成功故事。

Over the last decade (and even prior to that) CPPIB has been the best performing fund of its kind. National pension funds have different risk tolerances and investment guidelines that someone's personal portfolio or a family office.

Thanks to CPPIB, Canada does not have have a giant unfunded pension liability (unlike our neighbors to the south). It has been an enormous success story.

在 HN 中查看
sefrost 2026/04/28 01:59
是的，这比英国管理国家养老金制度的方式要好得多。

不过，澳大利亚人似乎拥有总体上最好的模式。对私人投资的强制付款使他们变得非常富有。

英国的制度收取工人的国家保险缴款，但不会代表个人将其投资于任何东西。因此，尽管支付了数十年的费用，从技术上讲，你最终什么也没有，只能依靠政府和当前纳税人的善意生存。由于人口金字塔，这种方法现在有效。

加拿大肯定有比这更好的系统。

Yes, far better than how the UK runs its state pension system.

The Australians seem to have the best model overall though. Mandatory payments in to private investments has made them very wealthy.

The UK system takes the national insurance contributions of workers but doesn’t invest them in anything on behalf of the individual. So despite decades of payments you technically have nothing at the end and survive on the goodwill of the government and current taxpayers. That works right now because of the population pyramid.

Canada definitely has a better system than that.

在 HN 中查看
mapt 2026/04/28 01:34
有多种类型的主权财富基金与资源开采税相关。 你在挪威看到的这种类型是用来保护经济多样性、货币和劳动力免受采掘业的沉重影响的。 你在沙特阿拉伯看到的这种类型，没有这样的多样性，基本上所有就业都与石油有一定程度的分离，基本上是长期基础设施项目的行贿基金，这些项目由于其规模与世俗经济的关系而不会获得批准，无论这些项目是港口等盈利性企业，还是道路等非营利性企业。

两人的目标都是把今天的意外之财花在妓女和吹牛之外的事情上。

挪威确实仍然有一些完全不合理的热情项目，比如它正在建设的沿海高速公路，但它是从普通基金中这样做的，目的是将财富基金作为一大堆投资资金单独管理，而这些资金恰好属于一个名为“挪威”的投资者，而在沙特阿拉伯，它是一种政策工具。

There are multiple types of sovereign wealth fund associated with taxes on resource extraction. The type you see in Norway is used to shelter the economic diversity, currency, and labor force from the steamrolling impact of the extractive industry. The type you see in Saudi Arabia, which has no such diversity and where basically all employment is tied to oil through few degrees of separation, is basically a slush fund for long-term infrastructure projects that would not otherwise be approved because of their size in relation to the secular economy, whether those are profit-generating enterprises like harbors or not-for-profit ones like roads.

Both aim to take today's windfall and spend it on something other than hookers and blow.

Norway still does have some totally unjustifiable passion projects, like the coastal highway it's building, but it's doing this from general funds to keep the wealth fund separately managed as a giant pile of investment money that just happens to belong to an investor called 'Norway', while in Saudi Arabia it is an instrument of policy.

在 HN 中查看
scosman 2026/04/28 01:26
我认为本文的主题不仅仅是回报。在国内投资一美元可获得 7% 的回报，再加上经济中增加一美元应税收入（以及税基的一些经常性收益），这比在国际上投资获得 9% 的一次性回报要好。
I think the thesis is it's not solely about returns. Get a 7% return investing a dollar domestically, plus add a taxable dollar to economy (and some recurring benefit to tax base) beats getting a 9% one-time return investing internationally.

llm_nerd 2026/04/28 01:50
>主权财富基金通常由超额利润提供资金，例如挪威。

如果加拿大像挪威那样经营资源，它将获得巨大的“超额利润”。挪威的特许权使用费和“利润”远远高于加拿大，几十年来美国心理医生欺骗了一群非常愚蠢的人，加拿大的主要目的是确保美国组织的最大利润。

但实际上，国际经济学大部分都是虚构的，如果有足够多的人认同它，那么它就非常真实了。

>Usually a sovereign wealth fund is funded by excess profits, like Norway for example.

If Canada ran resources like Norway does, it would have an enormous "excess profit". Norway's royalty rates and "profits" are dramatically higher than Canada where decades of American psyops fooled a bunch of very foolish people that the primary purpose of Canada is to ensure maximum profits for US orgs.

But really, international economics is just mostly made up, and if enough people go along with it then it's as real as real can be.

在 HN 中查看
lotsofpulp 2026/04/28 02:51
>但实际上，国际经济学大部分都是虚构的，如果有足够多的人认同它，那么它就非常真实了。

没有弥补的是，如果你需要从其他国家进口东西，那么你需要按比例从你的国家出口东西，否则整个国家就会失去购买力（即变得更穷）。 在这种情况下，如果加拿大增加货币供应量，那么货币的购买力将会下降，除非相应地增加对其货币的需求（通常通过增加对其商品和服务的需求，包括加拿大的土地或企业）。

>But really, international economics is just mostly made up, and if enough people go along with it then it's as real as real can be.

What is not made up is that if you need to import things from other countries, then you need to export things from your country in proportional value, or else the country as a whole loses purchasing power (i.e. gets poorer). In this case, if Canada is increasing its money supply, then the purchasing power of the currency will go down unless it correspondingly increases demand for its currency (usually by increased demand for its goods and services, including land or businesses in Canada).

在 HN 中查看
llm_nerd 2026/04/28 04:41
>没有弥补的是，如果你需要从其他国家进口东西，那么你需要按比例从你的国家出口东西，否则整个国家就会失去购买力（即变得更穷）。

那么到目前为止，美国是世界上最贫穷的国家，对吧？该国拥有超过 2 万亿美元的赤字，1 万亿美元的贸易赤字，绝对看不到螺旋式破产的迹象（情况只会变得更糟），并让印钞机继续运转。

大多数国际经济学都是 捏造，而且常常是无意义的。没有一本记录因果关系的主簿（根本就没有，所以我们最多只能得到“但是如果你这样做……那也许会发生……或者也许不会发生”，但大多数情况下，人们环顾四周，试图弄清楚其他人会同意什么。

>What is not made up is that if you need to import things from other countries, then you need to export things from your country in proportional value, or else the country as a whole loses purchasing power (i.e. gets poorer).

So the US is the world's poorest nation, by far, right? Country has a two trillion dollar+ deficit, a one trillion dollar trade deficit, absolutely no end in sight of spiralling to bankruptcy (it's only getting worse), and lets the money printer go brrrr.

Most of international economics are made up, and often are nonsensical. There is no master book of records that dictates cause and effect (there simply isn't, so at best we get "but if you do this...that maybe will happen...or maybe it won't", but mostly it's people looking around and trying to figure out what other people will go along with.

在 HN 中查看
lotsofpulp 2026/04/28 05:06
>那么到目前为止，美国是世界上最贫穷的国家，对吧？

考虑到美元仍然具有不错的购买力，不是吗？ 相对于其他国家，对美国商品和服务的需求可能已从峰值回落，但仍远高于其他国家。

>没有主记录簿来规定因果关系

有一些简单的东西，比如供给和需求。 无法回避的是，成功的和平贸易需要双方拥有对方想要的东西。

在民族国家层面上，以国家货币计价的债务更像是对一个国家未来生产力的要求（因为任何国家总是可以印钞或编辑数字数据库来偿还其债务）。

当你购买美国国债时，你就押注在未来的某个时候，美国将出售值得拥有的东西。 你并不担心你给美国的贷款会违约，因为美国政府用美元偿还你是小事一桩。 你的风险是，当它得到偿还时，你能用它买什么？

>So the US is the world's poorest nation, by far, right?

Considering the USD still has decent purchasing power, no? The demand for US goods and services relative to other nations' might have dropped from its peak, but still considerably higher than other nations.

>There is no master book of records that dictates cause and effect

There is for simple stuff, like supply and demand. There is no way of getting around that executing a successful peaceful trade requires both parties to have something that the other wants.

On a nation state level, debt denominated in a nation's currency is more like a claim on the future productivity of a nation (since any nation can always print money or edit a digital database to satisfy its debts).

When you buy a US Treasury, you are betting that at some point in the future, the US is going to be be selling things worth having. You are not worried that your loan to the US will default, because it is trivial for the US government to repay you in USD. Your risk is that when it gets paid back, what will you be able to buy with it?

在 HN 中查看
llm_nerd 2026/04/28 05:30
>考虑到美元仍然具有不错的购买力，不是吗？

你知道我正在申请 你的 逻辑，对吗？美国的贸易逆差绝对是全球最严重的。它买的东西远远多于卖的东西。它在地球上的主要角色是印钞机。

>当您购买美国国债时，您是在押注在未来的某个时候，美国将出售值得拥有的东西。

同样，这听起来几乎是二年级的。当您购买美国国债时，您是在押注美国在其到期时或在您提前抛售时仍会印钞。它不是基于合理的理性逻辑——如果是这样的话，这个拥有40万亿美元债务和2万亿美元赤字以及巨额贸易赤字的国家——就已经破产了。

当有人指出像特斯拉这样的股票是多么滑稽时，我觉得我正在和一个争论理性经济学的人争论。世界上所有的热议都改变不了一个事实，那就是它依赖于共同的错觉，每个人都在玩一场“胆小鬼”游戏。

>Considering the USD still has decent purchasing power, no?

You understand I was applying your logic, right? The US has the worst trade deficit on the planet, by a long shot. It buys far, far more than it sells. Its primary role on the planet is money printer.

>When you buy a US Treasury, you are betting that at some point in the future, the US is going to be be selling things worth having.

Again, this almost sounds sophomoric. When you buy US treasuries, you are betting that the US will still be printing money when it matures, or when you unload it earlier. It is not based on sound rational logic -- if so the country with the $40 trillion dollar debt and $2 trillion dollar deficit with the massive trade deficit -- would be already bankrupt.

I feel like I'm arguing with someone who is arguing rational economics when someone points out how farcical a stock like TSLA is. All of the hot air in the world doesn't change the fact that it relies upon a shared delusion, and everyone is playing a bit of a game of chicken.

在 HN 中查看
lotsofpulp 2026/04/28 05:59
>你明白我正在应用你的逻辑，对吗？

我不确定你指的是什么逻辑。 我的第一个回复是指出，无论“编造”的经济是什么，当紧要关头，都必须提供真实的商品和服务才能让这个谜题继续下去。 “足够多”的人不会与那些不能让他们得到他们想要的东西的人合作，如果他们认为货币不能让他们得到他们想要的东西，那么没有人会想要一种货币。

我的第二篇文章是对美国是最贫穷国家的说法的回应，但显然它不是，因为它仍然可以购买几乎任何它想要的东西。

不管技术细节如何，能买东西的人比买不到东西的人更富有。 也许这是由于共同的错觉，也许每个人都在扮演胆怯的角色。 显然，这只有在未来才会被揭示，但总的来说，这就是所有主权国家的债务，一种“错觉”（或假设），即国家生产力的成果在未来是值得拥有的。

>You understand I was applying your logic, right?

I am not sure what logic you are referring to. My first reply was to point out that no matter what economics are "made up", when push comes to shove, there has to be delivery of real goods and services for the charade to continue. "Enough" people won't go along with someone that doesn't result in them getting what they want, and no one wants a currency if they don't think it will get them what they want.

My second post is responding to the claim that America is the poorest country, to which it obviously isn't, since it can still buy almost whatever it wants.

Regardless of the technical details, someone who can buy something is richer than someone who cannot buy something. Maybe that is due to a shared delusion, and maybe everyone is playing a bit of chicken. Obviously, that will only be revealed in the future, but in general, that's what all sovereign nations' debt is, a "delusion" (or assumption) that the fruit of the nation's productivity will be worth having in the future.

在 HN 中查看
somewhereoutth 2026/04/28 01:29
我认为财富税（实际上是财富归还）应该纳入主权财富基金——尤其是这样公众可以看到这些钱如何为他们服务，从而支持此类税收的继续和扩大。
I believe wealth taxes (really, wealth restitution) should go into sovereign wealth funds - not least as then the public can see how that money is working for them, and so support the continuance and expansion of such taxes.

vizzier 2026/04/28 01:36

Agreed, we should also nationalise resource extraction and put the funds in there. Canadian resources should be for Canadians.

qball 2026/04/28 02:17
不。

从历史上看，这最终导致多伦多和蒙特利尔（以及在更有限的程度上温哥华）将全国其他地区视为资源殖民地。 假装被统治者的同意在地理上是平等分布的，这自然对你非常有用。

如果你再这样做，就像你在 60 年代所做的那样，加拿大只会 是 多伦多和蒙特利尔。

No.

Historically, this just ends up with Toronto and Montreal (and to a more limited extent, Vancouver) treating the rest of the country as a resource colony. The pretense that consent of the governed is equally geographically distributed is, naturally, very useful to you.

If you do that again, as you did in the '60s, Canada will only be Toronto and Montreal.

更重要的是——基金收入应用于减少所得税。这就是财富税获得广泛公众支持的方式。
More importantly - revenues from the funds should be used for reducing income taxes. That's how you get broad-based public support for wealth taxes.
